summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Nium targets stablecoins, growth
articles.description: The cross-border payments company bought Cypher this month to roll forward its stablecoin strategy, says CEO Prajit Nanu.

articles.title: 5 payments podcasts for a listen
articles.description: With summer in full swing, it’s a good time to head to the beach, take a walk or just lounge with an audio update on industry happenings.

articles.title: Ramp, FIS make AI moves
articles.description: The two payments companies this month embarked on separate artificial intelligence initiatives, with one focused on managing AI spending and the other using the technology to mitigate cybersecurity risks.

articles.title: Oregon proposes BNPL rules
articles.description: The state’s proposed rules would require buy now, pay later firms to obtain a payday or consumer finance license. A trade group countered the proposal.

articles.title: Payments broker gets jail time
articles.description: The fraudster, who created sham online marketing companies to bilk consumers and businesses, was sentenced to three years in prison Monday.

articles.title: Payments M&A spools up
articles.description: Stripe’s potential bid for PayPal isn’t the only merger and acquisition activity underway as deal-making revs up for the second half of the year.

articles.title: Why pricey credit cards may soon cost more
articles.description: A settlement over swipe fees could mark a major shift for retail payments as merchants embrace surcharges on high-end cards.

articles.title: Retail media was the first wave. Financial services may define the next one.
articles.description: Retail media changed advertising. Financial services could define what comes next.
